pattern: A single left-to-right stack pass catches every cancellation, including ones only created after an earlier removal, because a pop can expose a new adjacent match.
hints: Process the string left to right while keeping a stack of the characters that haven't been cancelled yet.
Before pushing the current character, check whether it would complete 'AB' or 'CD' with whatever is on top of the stack -- if so, pop instead of pushing.
Whatever remains on the stack after one pass is already the minimum length, because popping can immediately expose a new adjacent pair, and the stack check catches it on the very next character.

edge_inputs.description: cascading removals down to two characters
a pop exposes a second removable pair
fully cancels down to the empty string

stress.description: random uppercase strings biased toward A, B, C, D
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    pool = list('ABCD') * 4 + list('EFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ')
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
